jokes.text: Bender singing 'Going through the car wash' while apparently going through some kind of wash
jokes.setup: Robot character singing about mundane activity
jokes.punchline: The incongruity of a robot enjoying/singing about car wash
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate absurdity - Bender enjoying simple pleasures fits his hedonistic robot personality

jokes.text: Elzar's cooking show mentions 'give it a blast from your spice weasel. Bam!'
jokes.setup: Parody of celebrity chef shows
jokes.punchline: The absurd 'spice weasel' ingredient
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist invention - 'spice weasel' is memorably ridiculous and perfectly captures both futuristic weirdness and cooking show parody

jokes.text: Fry asks what Bender's watching, Bender denies cooking show and claims 'It was porno. Yeah, that's it!'
jokes.setup: Bender caught watching cooking show
jokes.punchline: He's more embarrassed about cooking than porn
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's toxic masculinity makes him prefer being seen as perverted than domestic

jokes.text: When Leela says she likes cooking too, Bender immediately calls her 'Pansy'
jokes.setup: Bender just admitted to liking cooking
jokes.punchline: He immediately insults Leela for the same thing
jokes.explanation: Sharp character consistency - Bender's hypocritical machismo in one word

jokes.text: Fry complains 'You call that a couch? I demand a pillow!'
jokes.setup: Fry being called out for laziness
jokes.punchline: His demands are somehow even more minimal than expected
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - instead of defending his work ethic, he makes an even lazier demand

jokes.text: Leela points out Bender is 'a robot without a sense of taste'
jokes.setup: Bender wants to be ship's cook
jokes.punchline: The obvious flaw in this plan
jokes.explanation: Solid logical humor pointing out the absurdity, though somewhat obvious

jokes.text: Bender retorts 'I wouldn't talk about taste, wearing a lime-green tank top'
jokes.setup: Leela criticizes Bender's lack of taste
jokes.punchline: Bender deflects to her fashion sense
jokes.explanation: Good comeback structure, stays in character with Bender's deflection tactics

jokes.text: 'Z is better. It is two more than X.' regarding Z-ray vs X-ray
jokes.setup: Vendor trying to sell X-ray that's mislabeled as Z-ray
jokes.punchline: Hilariously flawed logic about letter superiority
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - the mathematical reasoning applied to alphabetical order is perfectly stupid and memorable

jokes.text: Vendor warns Fry 'You're not in the 20th century. You don't know how things work' to which Fry responds 'I'm not a kid'
jokes.setup: Vendor giving Fry life advice about adapting
jokes.punchline: Fry completely misses the point, thinks it's about his age
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's obliviousness to deeper meanings

jokes.text: Fry claims 'I grew up in this city. These are my people' then awkwardly greets aliens with 'What up?' 'Word.'
jokes.setup: Fry claiming street cred with aliens
jokes.punchline: His painfully dated and inappropriate greeting attempts
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Fry's cultural disconnect and overconfidence create secondhand embarrassment

jokes.text: When told swamp root 'makes you irresistible to the opposite sex', Fry says 'That's ridiculous. I'll take two pounds'
jokes.setup: Fry dismissing the love potion claim
jokes.punchline: Immediately buying a large quantity anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy - the contradiction between his words and actions is immediate and clear

jokes.text: About purple slug causing diarrhea, Fry says 'Either one is fine'
jokes.setup: Being warned about intestinal distress
jokes.punchline: Fry's complete indifference to the consequence
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Fry's careless attitude toward consequences

jokes.text: The rapid-fire organ trading scene with lungs, gills, and 'Can't imagine why I would' need lungs
jokes.setup: Casual organ trading conversation
jokes.punchline: The matter-of-fact disposal of essential organs
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi absurdism showing casual attitude toward body modification

jokes.text: Professor's 'Good news' followed by Fry saying 'I don't like the sound of that!'
jokes.setup: Professor's habitual 'good news' opener
jokes.punchline: Fry's learned to fear these announcements
jokes.explanation: Effective running gag that builds character history - Fry has learned to fear the Professor's missions

jokes.text: Professor's escalating zone descriptions: 'Death Zone', 'Zone of No Return', 'Galaxy of Terror'
jokes.setup: Trying to reassure about the 'Forbidden Zone'
jokes.punchline: Each example is progressively more ominous
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure where each 'reassuring' example makes things sound worse

jokes.text: After tasting Bender's salty cooking: 'That's the saltiest thing I ever tasted. I once ate a bowl of salt.'
jokes.setup: Complaining about oversalted food
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to literally eating pure salt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke with perfect absurdist logic - if you've eaten pure salt, this food being saltier is genuinely impressive

jokes.text: Leela's extreme reaction to Bender's cooking: 'Oh, dear God!'
jokes.setup: Crew trying to be supportive of Bender's first meal
jokes.punchline: Leela's involuntary horrified reaction despite trying to be nice
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat showing the gap between intention and reality

jokes.text: Bender's defensive 'The salt level was 10% less than a lethal dose'
jokes.setup: Defending his terrible cooking
jokes.punchline: Using near-lethality as a quality metric
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - treating near-fatal as a cooking achievement rather than a problem

jokes.text: Fry burping after claiming innocence about drinking the emperor
jokes.setup: Fry denies drinking the emperor
jokes.punchline: His immediate burp completely undermines his denial
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy timing - the burp is perfect comedic evidence of guilt

jokes.text: Fry's logic: 'Chances were equally good it was an emperor'
jokes.setup: Leela pointing out he drank strange liquid
jokes.punchline: Fry's bizarre probability assessment
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist Fry logic - treating poison vs emperor as a 50/50 probability shows his unique worldview

jokes.text: Fry choosing Bender as prime minister: 'In your face, Gorgak'
jokes.setup: Serious political appointment decision
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a playground victory
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's immaturity in positions of power

jokes.text: Harem selection scene with Bender's reactions to Fry's choices
jokes.setup: Choosing a romantic partner
jokes.punchline: Fry's apparently unusual preferences shocking Bender
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that relies on unseen character designs, works but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: 'They are mild. You're soaking in one right now.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of how mild-mannered the people are
jokes.punchline: Revelation that they're literally made of liquid
jokes.explanation: Excellent double meaning pun - 'mild' applies to both personality and the liquid emperor's properties, with perfect setup/payoff

jokes.text: Flof's stand-up routine about people from different suns making different sounds
jokes.setup: Political satire about sun-based differences
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex social groups to sound effects
jokes.explanation: Decent parody of bad stand-up comedy, capturing the awkwardness of weak observational humor

jokes.text: Fry's response about reign length: 'I knew you wouldn't ask unless it was really high or low'
jokes.setup: Being asked to guess the average reign length
jokes.punchline: Meta-awareness of dramatic questioning tropes
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor showing character awareness of dramatic storytelling conventions - breaks fourth wall subtly

jokes.text: Fry's mangled fable: grasshopper burying acorns while octopus watches TV, ending with octopus getting race car
jokes.setup: Attempting to tell cautionary tale like grasshopper and ant
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled version with nonsensical moral
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of classic fable with perfect Fry-style confusion. The race car ending is memorably absurd and the mixed-up animals show his scattered knowledge

jokes.text: Fry's coronation: 'What up?' instead of formal oath opening
jokes.setup: Formal ceremonial moment requiring dignity
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual modern greeting
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Fry's consistent cultural disconnect, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Bender trying to make Fry cry: 'You're a pimple on society's ass! You'll never amount to anything!' Fry: 'I was emperor of a planet.'
jokes.setup: Bender insulting Fry's lack of achievement
jokes.punchline: Fry pointing out his literal royal status
jokes.explanation: Great ironic reversal - the insult fails because Fry has legitimate counterexample, showing how surreal his life has become

jokes.text: Bender's existential attack: 'Everyone you knew or loved in the 20th century is dead.' Fry: 'These things happen.'
jokes.setup: Devastating emotional truth about Fry's displacement
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual acceptance of cosmic tragedy
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - Fry's matter-of-fact response to universal loss shows both his resilience and emotional disconnection

jokes.text: 'There is no God, and your human ideals are laughable!' 'Phew! That's a load off my mind.'
jokes.setup: Ultimate nihilistic attack on meaning and purpose
jokes.punchline: Fry finding this liberating rather than devastating
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expected reaction - instead of despair, Fry feels relief at being freed from responsibility and meaning

jokes.text: Phew! That's a load off my mind
jokes.setup: Bender's devastating existential attack
jokes.punchline: Fry finds nihilistic despair relieving rather than upsetting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly subverts expectations - Fry's response to existential horror is relief, showing his unique psychological makeup

jokes.text: Bender's collect call: 'I won't give my name to you'
jokes.setup: Making a collect call requiring name identification
jokes.punchline: Refusing to give name while obviously being identifiable
jokes.explanation: Good Bender stubbornness - pointless rebellious act that defeats the purpose of the call

jokes.text: Bont's elaborate deception about Leela being dead followed by 'I said you were dead so he'd cry out the emperor'
jokes.setup: Dramatic description of Leela's apparent death and juicing
jokes.punchline: Casual admission it was all lies for practical purposes
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that builds emotion then deflates it pragmatically - effective structure for the crying plot

jokes.text: Leela's confused response: 'What are you talking about?' followed by Bont's explanation
jokes.setup: Audience and Fry believing Leela was killed
jokes.punchline: Revelation it was all manipulation, with Bont casually explaining the trick
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff with meta-commentary on manipulation

jokes.text: Everyone helping Fry cry by hurting him
jokes.setup: Fry needs to cry more to fully extract the emperor
jokes.punchline: The crew's solution is coordinated physical abuse as friendship
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing the crew's warped but effective approach to helping their friend

jokes.text: 'How we doing, Amy?' 'We're 1/10 of the way there.'
jokes.setup: Progress check on emotional extraction
jokes.punchline: Revealing they need much more crying
jokes.explanation: Functional plot humor showing the scale of tears needed, but not particularly memorable
